skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: AI-powered video creation agent that generates short videos (5-120 seconds) from natural language prompts. Handles scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music automatically.
skill.summary: Professional AI video generation skill for creating product ads, social media content, brand videos, and explainers in multiple aspect ratios.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 534 patterns across 13 files with risk score 100/100. After semantic evaluation, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES representing legitimate shell automation patterns. The skill is an API client for Pexo video service using standard bash scripting. Shell command substitution, network requests to pexo.ai, config file access (~/.pexo/config), and environment variable usage (PEXO_API_KEY) are all expected behaviors for this type of tool. No malicious patterns, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection detected.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution in Automation Scripts
low_findings.description: Multiple shell scripts use command substitution $(...) and temp file creation with mktemp. This is standard bash automation pattern, not a vulnerability. All command arguments are hardcoded or derived from validated function parameters, not user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Command substitution used for mktemp, date, and dirname - all standard utilities with no user input injection vectors.

low_findings.title: Network Requests to External API
low_findings.description: Scripts make HTTPS requests to pexo.ai API endpoints. This is expected behavior for an API client skill. URLs are hardcoded to legitimate Pexo service endpoints, not user-controlled.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All network calls go to pexo.ai domain - the legitimate service this skill interfaces with. No suspicious endpoints detected.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Credential Access
low_findings.description: Skill reads PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL from ~/.pexo/config file or environment. This is standard credential management for API clients. Credentials are used only for authenticating to Pexo API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Credential access is limited to PEXO_API_KEY for API authentication. No credential exfiltration or leakage patterns detected.

low_findings.title: Configuration File in Home Directory
low_findings.description: Skill uses ~/.pexo/config for storing API credentials and base URL. This follows conventional Unix configuration file patterns. File is read-only accessed, not modified by scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard config file location following XDG conventions. Read-only access pattern with no write operations detected.

content.user_title: Create AI Videos from Natural Language
content.value_statement: Transform your video ideas into professional content without editing skills. Pexo Agent handles scriptwriting, composition, and production automatically through simple chat-based interaction.
actual_capabilities: Generate 5-120 second videos from text prompts in 15-20 minutes
Support multiple aspect ratios: 16:9 landscape, 9:16 vertical, 1:1 square
Upload and incorporate user images, videos, and audio files as references
Iterate on videos through conversational feedback and revisions
Preview multiple video options and select preferred version
Deliver final video with downloadable URL and project link
limitations: Cannot crawl web URLs - files must be downloaded locally before upload
Each API call consumes credits from user account balance
Video production takes 15-20 minutes minimum for short videos
Requires valid Pexo API key and account configuration before use

use_cases.title: Marketing Content Creation
use_cases.description: Create product demonstration videos, brand commercials, and promotional content for social media platforms without hiring video production teams.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing managers and small business owners

use_cases.title: Social Media Video Production
use_cases.description: Generate TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts content quickly from simple text descriptions of the desired video concept.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Explainer Video Generation
use_cases.description: Produce educational and training videos by describing the topic and key points to cover, letting AI handle visual storytelling.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second product video for running shoes featuring dynamic movement shots and urban backgrounds.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick product showcase for e-commerce listing

prompt_templates.title: Brand Story Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 30-second brand introduction video with warm tones, showcasing our company values of sustainability and innovation.
prompt_templates.scenario: About us page or social media brand awareness

prompt_templates.title: Tutorial with Reference Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a tutorial video showing how to use this product <original-image>a_abc123xyz</original-image>. Focus on the key features demonstrated step by step.
prompt_templates.scenario: Product tutorial using uploaded reference image

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Revision Campaign Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Produce a 60-second campaign video for our summer sale. Start with energetic opening, show product benefits, end with call-to-action. I will provide feedback for revisions.

output_examples.input: Create a 15-second video showing a cat playing with yarn in a cozy living room
output_examples.output: Project submitted successfully. Estimated completion: 15-20 minutes. Project link: https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123

output_examples.input: Make a product video for these shoes <original-image>a_xyz789</original-image>
output_examples.output: Video production started. You will receive preview options to choose from. Current status: WAIT - AI is composing initial draft.

best_practices: Copy user messages exactly when relaying to Pexo - do not add your own creative interpretations
Always wrap uploaded asset IDs in proper tags like <original-image>asset_id</original-image>
Poll status every 60 seconds during WAIT state, never call chat endpoint while waiting
Send the full video URL with all query parameters to avoid 403 errors
Handle credit errors gracefully by guiding users to purchase page
anti_patterns: Do not add duration, aspect ratio, or style details the user did not mention - Pexo will ask if needed
Never call pexo-chat.sh during WAIT state - this triggers duplicate video production
Do not truncate video URLs - always include the complete URL with OSSAccessKeyId and Signature parameters
Avoid wrapping URLs in markdown links - send as plain text to prevent platform breakage

faq.question: What file formats does Pexo accept for uploads?
faq.answer: Images: jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic. Videos: mp4, mov, avi. Audio: mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac.

faq.question: How long does video production take?
faq.answer: Short videos (5-30 seconds) typically complete in 15-20 minutes. Longer or more complex videos may take additional time.

faq.question: What happens if I run out of credits?
faq.answer: The skill will notify you with a credit error message and provide a link to purchase more credits at pexo.ai/home.

faq.question: Can I revise a video after it is completed?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the same project_id and send feedback via pexo-chat.sh. The AI will generate revisions based on your input.

faq.question: Why can't Pexo use web URLs directly?
faq.answer: Pexo cannot crawl external URLs. Download files to your local system first, then upload them using pexo-upload.sh.

faq.question: What video aspect ratios are supported?
faq.answer: Three aspect ratios: 16:9 (landscape for YouTube), 9:16 (vertical for TikTok/Reels), and 1:1 (square for Instagram).
